zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Fuller於1397年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲時，他的父親Thomas Fuller23歲，母親Elizabeth Mobberly20歲。他於大約1425年與Margery Elene Yaxley在英國英格蘭素福爾可亞可利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1489年在他的家鄉享年92歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可聖 艾德蒙斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: Fuller
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a dresser of cloth, from Middle English fuller ‘fuller of cloth’ (partly from Old English fullere, partly from Old French fouleor, foleur, Latin fullo). Raw cloth had to be fulled, i.e. scoured, cleansed, and thickened by beating or trampling it in water, a process also known as walking or tucking, hence the surnames Walker and Tucker alongside Fuller. These three terms and surnames are characteristic of different parts of England. In general, in Middle English, Fuller is southern and eastern, while Walker belongs to the west and north and Tucker is southwestern. Compare Fullen .
English: variant of Fullard with loss of -d.
German (Füller): occupational name for a person whose work involved filling, such as a dauber, or a nickname for a gourmand or glutton. Compare Filler .

variants.name: Walker
meanings: English (mainly North and Midlands) and Scottish: occupational name for a fuller, from Middle English walker, Old English wealcere (an agent derivative of wealcan ‘to walk, tread’), ‘one who trampled cloth in a bath of lye or kneaded it, in order to strengthen it’. This was the regular term for the occupation during the Middle Ages in western and northern England. Compare Fuller and Tucker . As a Scottish surname it has also been used as a translation of Gaelic Mac an Fhucadair ‘son of the fuller’. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

History: The name was brought to North America from northern England and Scotland independently by many different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. Samuel Walker came to Lynn, MA, c. 1630; Philip Walker was in Rehoboth, MA, in or before 1643. The surname was also established in VA before 1650; a Thomas Walker, born in 1715 in King and Queen County, VA, was a physician, soldier, and explorer.

variants.name: Filler
meanings: South German: occupational name for a flayer or skinner, from Middle High German viller ‘knacker’, from villen ‘to skin’.

variants.name: Fullen
meanings: English: occupational name for a fuller (see Fuller ), from Old French fulun ‘fuller’.
English (Lancashire): branch of this family in Lancashire probably represents a variant of Fulham (see Fullam ), reintroduced from Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Fuller was born in 1397, in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Fuller, was 23 and his mother, Elizabeth Mobberly, was 20. He married Margery Elene Yaxley about 1425, in Yaxley, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1489, in his hometown, at the age of 92, and was buried in Saint Edmundsbury, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

ko.Bury St Edmunds, St Edmundsbury Borough,: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Fuller(이)가 1397년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Fuller(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Mobberly(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1425년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 야스리에서 Margery Elene Yaxley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1489년 그의 고향에서 92세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 세인트 에드먼드스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Fuller nasceu em 1397, em Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas Fuller, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Mobberly, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Margery Elene Yaxley aproximadamente 1425, em Yaxley, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1489, em sua cidade natal, com 92 anos, e foi sepultado em Saint Edmundsbury, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St Edmunds Abbey Bury St Edmunds, St Ed: бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Fuller родился в 1397 г. в местности бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thomas Fuller было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Mobberly, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery Elene Yaxley около 1425 г. в местности яксли, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1489 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 92 лет и был похоронен в местности сеинт эдмундсбури, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Margery Elene Yaxley aproximadamente 1425, en Yaxley, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1489, en su pueblo, a la edad de 92 años, y fue sepultado en Saint Edmundsbury, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
